data.title: Tango Therapeutics 2025 10-K: $62.4M Revenue, $(101.6)M Net Loss
data.description:
Tango Therapeutics reported $62.4 million of total revenue for the year, driven by recognition of remaining deferred collaboration revenue from Gilead, and recorded a net loss of $(101.6) million. Operating loss improved to $(111.3) million versus the prior year, reflecting lower R&D and G&A expenses, while interest income declined to $5.6 million. The company highlighted clinical progress in MTAP-deleted and CNS programs and strategic shifts in program prioritization and collaborations supporting runway into 2028.

Financial Highlights

  • Collaboration revenue: $62.4 million, an increase of $32.4 million versus 2024 due to recognition of remaining deferred revenue from Gilead.
  • Total revenue: $62.4 million, up $20.3 million versus 2024 driven by collaboration revenue recognition.
  • Loss from operations: $(111.3) million, an improvement of $34.3 million versus 2024 attributable to lower R&D and G&A expenses.
  • Net loss: $(101.6) million, improved $28.7 million versus 2024.
  • Interest income: $5.6 million, a decrease of $2.3 million versus 2024, impacting net results.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical milestones: Positive Phase 1/2 data for vopimetostat in MTAP-deleted cancers with a planned 2L pancreatic pivotal in 2026.
  • Pipeline advancement: TNG456, a brain-penetrant PRMT5 candidate, is in Phase 1/2 with a focus on glioblastoma (GBM); TNG961 is in IND-enabling studies.
  • Strategic collaborations: Completion of the Gilead research term and ongoing RAS combination trials through collaborations with Revolution and Erasca.
  • Revenue and funding shift: Recognition of collaboration revenue in 2025 following Gilead agreement truncation; financings and ATM programs intended to extend runway into 2028.
  • Operational prioritization: Discontinuation of TNG908 and TNG348 to reallocate resources toward higher-priority CNS and MTAP-deleted programs.

Original SEC Filing: Tango Therapeutics, Inc. [ TNGX ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Myers Industries 2025 10-K: $825.7M Revenue, $0.93 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Myers Industries reported fiscal 2025 net sales of $825.7 million and diluted net income per common share of $0.93, with revenue down modestly due to pricing and volume pressure while earnings improved on margin gains and a lower effective tax rate. The company's operating income rose significantly to $74.6 million, reflecting acquisition contribution and cost productivity. Improved gross margin and operational actions supported the year-over-year profitability recovery.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $825.7 million, down $10.5 million (1.3%) versus prior year; decline driven by lower pricing and volume.
  • Gross profit: $276.1 million, up $5.2 million (1.9%); gross margin 33.4% versus 32.4% prior year, attributed to improved productivity and lower material costs.
  • Operating income: $74.6 million, up $30.1 million (vs. $44.5 million in 2024); benefited from the acquisition and cost productivity.
  • Net income: $34.9 million, up from $7.2 million in 2024; effective tax rate 22.6% versus 46.8% prior year.
  • Net income per common share (Diluted): $0.93, versus $0.19 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Net sales modestly declined 1.3% in 2025, driven by price compression and slight volume weakness.
  • Segment performance: Material Handling was stable (approximately flat, +0.1%) aided by the Signature acquisition; Distribution declined 5.1% due to lower volume.
  • Acquisition and channel expansion: The February 2024 Signature acquisition contributed approximately $6.4 million of 2025 sales and expanded product breadth and channels.
  • Operational efficiency initiatives: Gross margin improvement was supported by cost productivity, lower material costs, and favorable mix.
  • Operational actions: Executed facility consolidations, restructurings, and asset disposals to improve cost structure and capacity utilization.

Original SEC Filing: MYERS INDUSTRIES INC [ MYE ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CPI Card Group 10-K: Revenue $543.5M, EPS $1.25
data.description:
CPI Card Group reported fiscal-year revenue of $543.5 million, up 13.1% year over year, driven in part by the acquisition of Arroweye; diluted earnings per share were $1.25, down from $1.64 as net income declined. The company noted pressure on margins and operating income from mix shifts toward lower-margin products and higher production and integration costs. Operational investments and a change in revenue recognition for certain prepaid activity also affected reported results.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $543.5 million, increased $62.9 million (13.1%) versus prior year; Arroweye contributed $42.8 million.
  • Gross Profit: $170.1 million, decreased $1.1 million (0.7%); gross margin declined to 31.3% from 35.6% due to unfavorable mix and higher production costs.
  • Income from operations: $54.8 million, decreased $7.95 million (12.7%) driven by higher SG&A and integration costs.
  • Net income: $15.0 million, decreased $4.6 million (23.4%), reflecting higher taxes and lower operating income.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.25, down from $1.64, reflecting lower net income and share count effects.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: The Arroweye acquisition (closed May 2025) materially expanded capabilities and contributed significant revenue; growth was also supported by higher contactless card volumes.
  • Product and channel mix: Increased mix of contactless and metal cards and higher Card@Once instant issuance sales shifted overall product mix toward those offerings.
  • Strategic investments: Completed a 20% strategic investment in Karta to support digital prepaid growth initiatives.
  • Operational capacity expansion: Invested $18.2 million in capital expenditures for a new Indiana production facility and recorded increased depreciation related to expanded capacity.
  • Accounting and reporting change: During Q2 2025 the company shifted certain work-in-process prepaid revenue to point-in-time recognition, reducing reported Prepaid Debit revenue under the prior method.

Original SEC Filing: CPI Card Group Inc. [ PMTS ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Dutch far-right lawmakers argue Groningen gas field shouldn't be permanently shut
data.description:
A far-right political party in the Netherlands said on Thursday it will propose a plan to keep the Groningen gas field, once Europe's largest, available as a strategic reserve despite its closure in 2024 due to seismic risks.

The JA21 party's plan would preserve the option to re-tap some wells, a move at odds with the Dutch government's earlier commitment to permanently seal the wells with concrete and never reopen them.

The proposal has gained support among other far-right lawmakers, including Geert Wilders of the Freedom Party.

"In the current geopolitical situation, keeping our own gas wells as an emergency reserve makes sense," Wilders said, adding that the gas field's output should remain exclusively for the Netherlands and not serve the rest of Europe.

Parliament has not endorsed the plan. Lawmakers who have come out in favor of the plan represent about 22% of votes.

The Groningen field, once a major gas supplier for Europe, ceased production entirely in 2024 following decades of extraction that caused earthquakes and widespread damage to homes in the region. The Dutch government has repeatedly ruled out reopening the field due to safety concerns.

data.title: Perceived Length of Energy Price Shock Is Key for Some European Currencies — Market Talk
data.description: A more sustained weakening of the currencies of European energy importer countries would likely require the market to price a longer disruption to natural gas prices resulting from the Middle East conflict, Goldman Sachs analysts say in a note. The European natural gas market is pricing this energy price shock as more temporary compared to the rise in oil and gas prices after Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022, they say. This would need to shift in order to see a more prolonged depreciation of European energy importer currencies including the euro, sterling, Hungarian forint, Polish zloty and Czech koruna, they say. The forint and zloty "screen as the most vulnerable to energy price upside." (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Spin Master Swings to 4Q Loss as Noncash Impairment Charge Weighs, Revenue Slips
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Spin Master swung to a loss in the fourth quarter due to a noncash impairment charge while revenue slumped due lower sales and higher promotional activity over the critical holiday period.

The toys and children's entertainment company posted on Thursday a net loss of $184.3 million, or $1.85 a share, compared with a profit of $21.1 million, or 21 cents a share, in the comparable quarter a year ago.

The loss is mainly due to a $229.1 million noncash impairment charge for the reduced value of assets on its balance sheet.

On an adjusted basis, which excludes exceptional items and one-off costs such as the impairment, earnings came to 41 cents a share. According to FactSet, analysts were expecting 37 cents a share.

The final three months of the year are a key period for toy retailers as holiday shopping drives a large share of annual sales, making the quarter among the industry's most critical revenue window.

The Toronto-based company, known for brands like PAW Patrol, Hatchimals and Rubik's Cube, generated 4.8% lower revenue, reaching $618.2 million, which wasn't as low as analysts expected at $605.4 million.

Toy revenue was lower in the quarter due to depressed sales and higher markdowns and promotional activities, while entertainment revenue also fell. Digital games' revenue bucked the trend, with revenue rising thanks to strategic distribution partnerships and higher in-game purchases.

Looking ahead, Spin Master expects flat to low-single-digit revenue growth for the full year, with analysts expecting $2.16 billion.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization are projected to rise mid to high-single digit percentage points.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Hungary will force Ukraine to reopen key pipeline for Russian oil, Orban says
data.description:
Hungary will force neighbouring Ukraine with "political and financial tools" to reopen the key Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil to Hungarian refineries, Prime Minister Viktor Orban said on Thursday.

Flows of Russian oil through the Druzhba to ​Hungary and Slovakia via Ukraine have been suspended since late January when Kyiv says the pipeline was seriously damaged by a fire after a Russian attack.

Hungary and ‌Slovakia, the only European ​Union countries still importing Russian oil, ⁠have accused Ukraine of deliberately delaying the resumption of oil flows for political reasons. Kyiv says the pipeline cannot be repaired quickly.

"I would like to make clear that we will win, and we will win with force," Orban told a business conference.

"We have no military force for this, I can reassure everyone that this is not part of our plans. But we have political and financial tools," he said, without elaborating.

Last month, Hungary vetoed new EU sanctions on Russia and also a huge loan for Ukraine over the Druzhba pipeline dispute.

In power since 2010, the veteran leader has made the Ukraine war a focal point in his campaign for re-election in an April 12 ballot, accusing his centre-right challenger Peter Magyar of plotting to drag Hungary into the war raging next door. Magyar strongly denies the suggestion.

With Hungary mired in a three-year-long economic stagnation, most polls show Magyar's Tisza party ahead by a wide margin, although the outcome of the vote remains uncertain.

"There will be no compromise whatsoever. We will defeat them, we will wrestle down the oil blockade and we will force the Ukrainians to resume shipments," Orban said.

data.title: Global markets take a breath
data.description:
By Mike Dolan

What matters in U.S. and global markets today

By Mike Dolan, Editor-At-Large, Finance and Markets

Asian markets snapped back on Thursday, with South Korea’s KOSPI rising by almost 10%, clawing back a chunk of its drubbing in recent days, and Japan’s Nikkei adding nearly 2%.

The apparent respite amid the ongoing Middle East conflict wasn’t confined to Asia, with U.S. and European stocks also pushing higher on Wednesday as investors took comfort from even the faintest hints that geopolitical tensions might ease.

I’ll get into that and more below.

But first, check out my latest column on why Europe may be able to absorb the Middle East energy shock - for now.

And listen to the latest episode of the Morning Bid daily podcast. Subscribe to hear Reuters journalists discuss the biggest news in markets and finance seven days a week.

GLOBAL MARKETS TAKE A BREATH

Global markets appear to be catching their breath. The Nasdaq jumped 1.29% on Wednesday in a tech-led rally, while the S&P 500 rose 0.78%, helped along by some upbeat macro prints.

A mix of factors seem to be fuelling this market breather. Traders seemed to take heart in the New York Times’ report yesterday that Iranian intelligence had indirectly reached out to the CIA after Saturday’s attacks to try to reach a resolution. Washington’s plan to help reopen the embattled Strait of Hormuz via navy escorts and insurance coverage may also have raised some optimism.

There was also some positive news on the labour market front ahead of Friday’s payrolls release. U.S. private payrolls printed a higher-than-expected rise for February. ISM's non-manufacturing PMI, meantime, hit a more than three-year high.

But uncertainty still reigns as the Middle East conflagration enters its sixth day and a swift resolution to a clogged-up Strait of Hormuz looks, for now, like a distant prospect.

Indeed, analysts have warned that Iran can keep up drone harassment in the narrow waterway for months, and it's far from clear that tankers would brave the strait even with U.S. naval and insurance support in place.

And other signs point towards this being more than a days-long conflict, as the U.S. Senate failed to block the administration’s campaign against Tehran, giving President Trump largely unbounded power to direct the war.

Oil marched higher on Thursday on the prospect of continued disruption. Both U.S. and Brent crude jumped over 2%, with the latter topping $83 per barrel.

Gold also edged up on safe-haven demand, and the dollar strengthened again after retreating from three-month highs.

Meantime, U.S. stock index futures edged lower ahead of the bell and U.S. Treasuries eased. Investors will have even more to chew on later today with the release of weekly jobless claims, ahead of tomorrow's non-farm payrolls.

Elsewhere, chip designer Broadcom on Wednesday reported a first-quarter revenue rise of 29% to $19.31 billion and projected its AI chip revenue would top $100 billion next year. It’s emerging as a strong competitor in the space as it signs deals to rival those of its dominant competitor Nvidia, including with Meta.

Chart of the day

Global stocks have rebounded in recent days, recovering some of their 2026 gains lost in the wake of the U.S.-Israel attack on Iran on 28 February.

Today's events to watch

* U.S. weekly jobless claims (8:30 AM EST), January import prices (8:30 AM EST), Q4 productivity (8:30 AM EST)

* Fed's Michelle Bowman speaks

Want to receive the Morning Bid in your inbox every weekday morning? Sign up for the newsletter here. You can find ROI on the Reuters website, and you can follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Opinions expressed are those of the author. They do not reflect the views of Reuters News, which, under the Trust Principles, is committed to integrity, independence, and freedom from bias.
